root_text.sn15.11:1.4: Pubbā koṭi na paññāyati avijjānīvaraṇānaṁ sattānaṁ taṇhāsaṁyojanānaṁ sandhāvataṁ saṁsarataṁ.
root_text.sn15.11:1.5: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, passeyyātha duggataṁ durūpetaṁ niṭṭhamettha gantabbaṁ:
root_text.sn15.11:1.8: yāvañcidaṁ, bhikkhave, alameva sabbasaṅkhāresu nibbindituṁ alaṁ virajjituṁ alaṁ vimuccitun”ti.

translation_text.sn15.11:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī.
translation_text.sn15.11:1.3: “Mendicants, this transmigration has no known beginning.
translation_text.sn15.11:1.4: No first point is found of sentient beings roaming and transmigrating, shrouded by ignorance and fettered by craving.
translation_text.sn15.11:1.5: When you see someone in a sorry state, in distress, you should conclude:
translation_text.sn15.11:1.6: ‘In all this long time, we too have undergone the same thing.’
translation_text.sn15.11:1.7: Why is that? This transmigration has no known beginning. …
translation_text.sn15.11:1.8: This is quite enough for you to become disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed regarding all conditions.”
